key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays but does not eliminate plasticity loss: even large models eventually become less able to learn new information.
engineering_relevance: For continual-learning systems, parameter scaling alone is not a reliable fix, so model design must also preserve adaptability over long training horizons.
method: Trained multilingual GPT-style Transformer models with 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters.
Evaluated plasticity using a held-out Vietnamese probing task during continual learning.
Also tested stationary multilingual training to see whether plasticity loss appears without abrupt task switches.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a measurable deterioration on the held-out Vietnamese probe across model sizes, with the onset of plasticity loss following a sublinear scaling law.
watch_item: Larger models may postpone the problem, but the study suggests they do not fully prevent plasticity loss, so long-run adaptability remains a concern.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
